title: Lemon and citric acid microwave cleaner
subtitle: A steam-cleaning method using citric acid and lemon essential oil to loosen baked-on splatter
excerpt: Heating water with citric acid in the microwave creates acidic steam that loosens baked-on food splatter and neutralises odours. After 5 minutes of steaming and 5 minutes of sitting, the interior wipes clean without scrubbing. Lemon essential oil leaves a fresh scent. One treatment takes about 12 minutes.
sourceNotes: Acidic steam cleaning: heating an acidic water solution in a microwave produces steam that condenses on the interior walls and ceiling. The moisture softens dried-on food; the acidity helps neutralise alkaline odour compounds from fats and proteins. Citric acid at 5% solution (15 g in 300 ml water): mild, food-safe acid that is GRAS. Lemon essential oil: fragrance only; citrus steam adds a clean scent and may have some mild antimicrobial effect on surfaces, though the primary cleaning mechanism is the moisture and heat, not the oil. Use a microwave-safe bowl; do not use sealed containers.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Prepare the solution and use immediately; there is no make-ahead benefit for a steam cleaning treatment.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: Make fresh each time. The solution is discarded after use.

glossaryTerms.definition: Steam produced by boiling a mildly acidic solution. When the steam condenses on cooler surfaces inside the microwave, the condensate is slightly acidic. The moisture softens dried-on food; the acidity assists in neutralising odour-causing alkaline compounds from fats and proteins.

content.text: The inside of a microwave picks up splattered fat and protein that bake on during subsequent uses. The steam cleaning method works by filling the interior with

: the moisture condenses on the dried splatter and softens it, and the acidity helps neutralise odours from cooked fats. After a 5-minute steam and a 5-minute rest with the door closed, the interior wipes clean with a damp cloth with very little effort. Lemon essential oil adds a clean citrus scent to the steam. The bowl will be very hot; use oven gloves when removing it.

items.prepNote: In a microwave-safe bowl; do not use a sealed container
About 5% of water weight; dissolve before heating
Add to water just before microwaving; fragrance only

content.text: Weigh the water into a microwave-safe bowl. Add the citric acid and stir until dissolved. Add the lemon essential oil. Place the bowl in the centre of the microwave. Microwave on full power until the solution comes to a vigorous boil and steam fills the interior, about 4 to 5 minutes. Do not open the door immediately.

Leave the door closed for 5 minutes after the microwave stops; the steam continues to work on the splatter as it cools slightly. Open the door carefully; the interior will be very hot and steamy. Remove the bowl using oven gloves. Wipe the interior walls, ceiling, and floor with a damp cloth or sponge. Dried splatter should wipe away with minimal effort. For stubborn spots, dip the cloth in the warm cleaning solution and scrub briefly.
Discard the solution after use. Make fresh for each cleaning session. No storage required.
